- why-most-digital-transformations-fail: kept Software Engineering
- copq-in-software-engineering: kept Quality Engineering

Category distribution now: QE=8, Test Automation=7, Soft Eng=4, Security=2

commit.message:
Initial commit

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Two SRE Agents: sre-zavapower-ops + sre-zavapower-itsupport

Replaced single sre-powergrid with two purpose-built agents:

sre-zavapower-ops (Infrastructure Operations):
  - Incident source: Azure Monitor alerts + Response Plans
  - SNOW integration: Python tools (create/update/resolve via REST API)
  - 4 tools, 3 subagents, 7 skills, 2 knowledge docs, 1 response plan
  - Handles: disk pressure, app crashes, perf regression, config errors

sre-zavapower-itsupport (IT Helpdesk):
  - Incident source: ServiceNow native (polls SNOW for new tickets)
  - SNOW integration: Built-in native tools
  - 2 tools (CheckWarranty, LookupServiceNowIncident)
  - 1 subagent (it-support-handler)
  - Handles: laptop replacements, warranty checks, form filling

Manual step for itsupport agent: connect ServiceNow as Incident
Platform in sre.azure.com portal (Basic auth to dev268981).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: import hourly statistics for Energy Dashboard

Major refactor to properly support the Energy Dashboard:

- Import hourly statistics via async_add_external_statistics
  (96 x 15-min values aggregated into 24 hourly entries per day)
- Initialize cumulative sum from recorder on startup (replaces
  RestoreEntity approach — no more race conditions)
- Change update interval from 30min to 4h (smart meter transmits
  full day data once per day, frequent polling is unnecessary)
- Add after_dependencies for recorder in manifest
- Simplify sensors: remove RestoreEntity, extra_state_attributes

Energy Dashboard setup: add external statistic
'evn_smartmeter:energy_consumption' as grid consumption source.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix layout: override screen min-height + ResizeObserver canvas sizing

Root causes:
1. #screen-game inherited .screen { min-height: 100vh } — the game-layout
   (height: 100vh) was inside a potentially-taller container, breaking the
   flex column constraints and allowing content to overflow below the fold.
   Game body appeared empty (overflow:hidden clipped it) until sidebar
   opened and forced a reflow. Fix: #screen-game { height:100vh;
   overflow:hidden; min-height:unset }.

2. CSS height:min(100%,420px) on canvas — inside a flex column whose
   height comes from cross-axis stretch, 100% resolves inconsistently
   across browsers and collapses to 0 when sidebar width changes.
   Fix: removed all CSS sizing from #map-canvas; width/height are set
   imperatively by a ResizeObserver watching .map-panel.

3. No resize handling on sidebar toggle — even if initial size was right,
   the 200px sidebar slide-in shrank the map-panel without updating the
   canvas. Fix: ResizeObserver fires on every panel size change
   (including during the CSS transition), plus an explicit redraw 270ms
   after toggle (after the 250ms transition settles).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use node:22-slim stage to pre-install Playwright Chromium

The bundled-node approach (.playwright/node/linux-x64/node) was crashing
at Docker build time with exit code 1 after 66ms. Root cause unclear but
likely a permissions or ELF issue with the unpacked NuGet binary in the
SDK container.

Replace with a dedicated playwright-browsers stage using node:22-slim
(Debian/glibc — same libc as the final aspnet:10.0 image). This ensures:
- npx playwright@1.58.0 install chromium downloads the exact browser
  revision expected by Microsoft.Playwright NuGet 1.58.0
- The binary is built for glibc (not musl), matching the runtime container
- No PowerShell, no bundled-node path hacks — just npm tooling

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Validates thesis-driven design rules:
- Trend-followers have DIRECTION_BIAS in trending regimes
- Dangerous regimes (TREND_DOWN, HIGH_VOL_CHOP) have tightened params
- CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD = baseline + 4 in dangerous regimes
- MAX_OPEN_POSITIONS_PER_PASSPORT never exceeds passport baseline
- Mean-reversion and breakout have no DIRECTION_BIAS

commit.message:
Add FCM data message support

- Install firebase-admin SDK
- Add device_tokens table (fcm_token PK, user_id FK)
- POST /api/device-tokens — register an FCM token for a user
- sendFcmDataMessages(userId, payload) — fans out FCM data messages
  to all registered devices for a user; auto-removes invalid tokens
- Wire FCM into DELETE /api/notifications/:id → { type: 'dismiss', notificationId }
- Wire FCM into POST /api/notifications/:id/actions → { type: 'action', notificationId, actionTaken[, actionResponse] }
- Initialise Firebase Admin via secrets.fcm.serviceAccount (gracefully
  degraded when not configured)
- Clean up device_tokens in pruneInactiveUsers and reset-confirm
- Document FCM service account config in secrets.example.json

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(frontend): auditoria UI/UX completa — 5 fases

- Fase 1: 4 bugs corrigidos (var CSS alias, API method buscarKpis, toast warning, CMV sort)
- Fase 2: emojis -> Lucide icons (15 estaticos + renderCatalogo/Config/openModal)

- Fase 4: performance (ResizeObserver RAF throttle, onFilter debounce 150ms)
- Fase 5: dark mode completo (11 overrides: modal, botoes, badges, scrollbar, sidebar)

commit.message:
[ci-coach] ci: concurrency control, job timeouts, and fix JS glob coverage (#7)

* ci: add concurrency control, timeouts, and fix JS glob coverage

- Add concurrency group to cancel superseded in-progress runs; avoids
  wasted compute minutes when commits are pushed rapidly to the same
  branch or PR
- Add timeout-minutes per job (15 min C#, 5 min JS/Python) to prevent
  hung builds from consuming unbounded runner time
- Fix JavaScript validation glob: replace Solutions/JavaScript/*.js
  shell glob with recursive find so subdirectory files are also checked
  (The-Gridlock-Arena-of-Mythos/ and The-Knowledge-Cartographer-Agent-MCP/
  were previously silently skipped — 4 JS files uncovered)
- Add top-level permissions: contents: read for minimal privilege

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

commit.message:
fix: close follow-up review items

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: populate regime_params for all 26 passports (Phase 2)

Thesis-driven per-regime parameter tuning:
- Trend-followers: LONG_ONLY in TREND_UP, SHORT_ONLY + tightened params in TREND_DOWN
- Mean-reversion: higher confidence + lower risk in HIGH_VOL_CHOP
- Breakout: tightened params in TREND_DOWN only
- Hybrids: individual treatment based on strategy thesis

Parameters adjusted per dangerous regime:
- CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD: baseline + 4
- RISK_PER_TRADE_PCT: 0.5 -> 0.3
- MAX_OPEN_POSITIONS_PER_PASSPORT: capped at 15 (TREND_DOWN) or 10 (HIGH_VOL_CHOP)
- DIRECTION_BIAS: LONG_ONLY/SHORT_ONLY for directional strategies

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
cs20: Authentication UX Overhaul (#146)

* feat(cs20): move auth to top bar, add profile logout, hide multiplayer, open leaderboard, add sign-in banner

- CS20-1: Persistent auth top bar with login/register or username/logout
- CS20-2: Logout button on profile screen
- CS20-3: Hide multiplayer button when not logged in
- CS20-4: Leaderboard endpoints use optionalAuth for anonymous access
- CS20-5: Non-blocking sign-in banner when starting game unauthenticated

Agent: yoga-gwn-c4/wt-1
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(cs20): update auth E2E tests for new top bar, multiplayer gating, leaderboard

- Rewrite auth.spec.mjs for top-bar auth flow (login/register/logout)
- Add tests for multiplayer button hiding, leaderboard anon access
- Add sign-in banner and profile logout tests
- Update leaderboard/submissions test helpers for new auth flow
- Add unit tests for unauthenticated leaderboard endpoints

Agent: yoga-gwn-c4/wt-1
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cs20): add unique IPs to E2E tests to avoid rate-limit failures

Adds X-Forwarded-For headers with unique IPs per test in auth, leaderboard,
and community spec files to prevent register endpoint rate-limit rejections
when tests run in sequence.

Agent: yoga-gwn-c4/wt-1
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cs20): address Copilot review feedback

- Remove data-action from sign-in banner link, add stopPropagation to
  prevent duplicate navigation from both the local handler and the
  global delegated click handler
- Insert sign-in banner after auth-top-bar instead of prepending to #app
  so it appears below the persistent auth controls
- Extract uniqueIP() into shared tests/e2e/helpers.mjs to eliminate
  duplication across auth, community, leaderboard, and submissions specs

Agent: yoga-gwn-c4/wt-1
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cs20): fix docstrings per review — uniqueIP scope, leaderboard period param

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cs20): address review round 3 — JSDoc mode, stable test selectors

- Update leaderboard JSDoc to include daily mode alongside freeplay

Agent: yoga-gwn-c4/wt-1
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cs20): normalize period default to alltime, set authReturnScreen in banner

- Change default period from 'all' to 'alltime' in both leaderboard
  endpoints to match client usage and JSDoc
- Set authReturnScreen to 'home' in sign-in banner link so users
  return to home (not multiplayer) after authentication
- Update unit test expectation for period default

Agent: yoga-gwn-c4/wt-1
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cs20): make logger resilient to missing pino-pretty in container

The Docker image omits devDependencies but docker-compose sets
NODE_ENV=development. Gracefully fall back to JSON output when
pino-pretty is not installed.

Agent: yoga-gwn-c4/wt-1
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(cs20): add HOST_PORT env var for Docker port isolation

Allows multiple agents and worktrees to run independent containers
by setting HOST_PORT and using docker compose -p for project isolation.
Default remains 3000 for backward compatibility.

fix: replace PowerShell with bundled Node.js for Playwright install

PowerShell is not in Ubuntu's default apt repos and requires Microsoft's
package feed to install. Instead, use the Node.js binary that the Playwright
.NET NuGet package bundles in the publish output (.playwright/node/linux-x64/).

playwright.ps1 is just a thin wrapper around this same Node binary — this
change calls it directly and removes the PowerShell dependency entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add crash types 27-29: ObjectAnimator, InnerClassReflection, AndroidOnClick

Type 27 - OBJECT_ANIMATOR_PROPERTY_RENAMED:
  ObjectAnimator.ofFloat(view, "propertyName") references a setter/getter
  by string. R8 renames the method → NoSuchMethodError at runtime.
  Detection: source pattern match + DEX/mapping confirmation that setter was renamed.
  Fix: -keepclassmembers with explicit setter/getter signatures.

Type 28 - INNER_CLASS_REFLECTION_RENAMED:
  Class.forName("Outer$Inner") uses $-notation for nested classes.
  R8 renames nested classes → ClassNotFoundException at runtime.
  Detection: InnerClassReflectionAnalyzer + ReflectionClassAnalyzer both detect the pattern.
  Fix: -keep class Outer { *; } + -keep class Outer$Inner { *; }

Type 29 - ANDROID_ONCLICK_METHOD_RENAMED:
  android:onClick="methodName" in layout XML resolves by reflection at runtime.
  R8 renames the handler method → NoSuchMethodException at runtime.
  Detection: AndroidOnClickAnalyzer scans layout XML for onClick attributes,
             cross-references method in source, confirms via mapping/DEX.
  Fix: -keep class ActivityOrFragment { public void handler(android.view.View); }

Changes:
- PostBuildAnalysisTask: XML scanning via resDirs, 3 new analyzer registrations,
  3 post-build confirmation branches
- ProguardProtectPlugin: populate resDirs from variant source sets
- ObjectAnimatorAnalyzer: fixed viewInheritPattern regex for constructor params
- ProguardIssue: 3 new IssueType enum entries
- HtmlReportGenerator: icons + labels for 3 new types
- proguard-rules.pro: fix rules for types 27-29 (commented out = crash mode)
- README: 29 total crash types, new type entries + example fixes

Verified: crash mode → 33 confirmed issues (types 27-29 detected)
          fix mode  → 30 confirmed issues (types 27-29 silenced, no false positives)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix game layout: min-height:0 on game-body, drop container queries for canvas

- .game-body gets min-height:0 so it can shrink inside the flex column;
  without it the default min-height:auto prevented shrinking and let
  content overflow the 100vh game-layout, pushing the map off-screen
- Removed container-type:size from .map-panel — cqh creates a layout
  cycle (element needs its own height to resolve the unit that sets it),
  causing the canvas to collapse and the map to appear ~20px tall
- New canvas sizing: height:min(100%,420px) + width:auto +
  aspect-ratio:1/1 + max-width:100%
  → height-first: fills panel height up to 420px
  → aspect-ratio derives width; max-width re-constrains if panel is
    narrower, browser resolves the square to min(H,W,420px) correctly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Enable autocompletion for arguments after alias commands

When users type an alias followed by a space (e.g., 'cs '), the completer
now expands the alias and provides completions for the expanded command's
arguments and subcommands.

Changes:
- Added _expand_alias_for_completion() method to resolve aliases to their
  actual command names
- Modified get_completions() to expand aliases before looking up command
  completions
- Users can now type 'cs ' and get completions for 'case show' subcommands

Example workflow:
  1. alias cs='case show'
  2. Type 'cs ' and press Tab
  3. See subcommands: show, create, run, organise, check, add, report, status

This completes the alias autocompletion feature by enabling intelligent
argument and subcommand completion for aliased commands.

commit.message:
docs: Phase 2 per-regime parameter tuning design spec

Thesis-driven approach: parameters set based on strategy logic per regime
character, organized by category (trend-following, mean-reversion, breakout,
hybrid).

Key decisions:
- DIRECTION_BIAS enforced for directional strategies (LONG_ONLY in TREND_UP,
  SHORT_ONLY in TREND_DOWN for trend-followers + momentum hybrids)
- CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD: baseline + 4 in dangerous regimes (TREND_DOWN,
  HIGH_VOL_CHOP)
- RISK_PER_TRADE_PCT: 0.3% in dangerous regimes (down from 0.5%)
- MAX_OPEN_POSITIONS_PER_PASSPORT: capped at 15 (TREND_DOWN) or 10
  (HIGH_VOL_CHOP), never exceeding passport baseline
- No changes to USE_TRAILING_STOP or ATR_TRAIL_MULTIPLIER

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ui): keep camera preview alive during minimal mode

Toggling minimal mode removed CameraPreview from the Compose tree,
which triggered surfaceDestroyed() -> stopPreview() on RootEncoder,
irreversibly stopping camera capture and killing the stream.

Fix: always keep CameraPreview in the composition tree. In minimal
mode, shrink it to 1dp (keeping the surface alive) and overlay the
MinimalStreamingOverlay on top. The encoder continues receiving
camera frames while the user sees the minimal UI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(service): add 1 Hz stats ticker for streaming duration timer

The streaming HUD timer was always showing 00:00 because durationMs in
StreamStats was never updated. No timer coroutine existed.

Add startStatsTicker()/stopStatsTicker() to StreamingService:
- Captures stream start time in onConnectionSuccess()
- Launches a coroutine that updates _streamStats.durationMs every 1s
- Cancels the ticker in cleanupAndStop()
- Resets stats to fresh StreamStats() on new startStream()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): treat cancelled jobs as failures in merge queue status check (#3972)

* fix(ci): treat cancelled jobs as failures in merge queue status check

The summarize-workflow action treated `cancelled` as an acceptable
result alongside `success` and `skipped`. This allowed the CI status
check to pass when upstream jobs (e.g. rate-limit-gate) were cancelled,
causing all downstream jobs to be  letting PRs through theskipped
merge queue without any checks actually running.

Remove `cancelled` from the pass-through case so it falls through to
the failure path, aligning the code with the README documentation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: Apply megalinter fixes

* Update .github/actions/summarize-workflow/action.yaml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Nikolai Emil Damm <ned@devantler.tech>

* docs: update summarize-workflow README to match new defaults

Sync default messages and description in README to reflect that
cancelled jobs are now treated as failures.

commit.message:
feat(kanban): animate cross-column card moves (#96)

## Summary
- normalize desktop kanban state into a shared scene model and pass it
into the UI board
- move kanban layout and cross-column motion ownership into @acepe/ui
with a board-level overlay and ghost card mode
- replace brittle source-text assertions with behavioral desktop tests
and pure UI motion/layout coverage

## Validation
- cd packages/website && bun run i18n:generate && bun run check
- cd packages/desktop && bun run check && bun test
src/lib/components/main-app-view/components/content/desktop-kanban-scene.test.ts
src/lib/components/main-app-view/components/content/kanban-view.test.ts
- cd packages/ui && bun x vitest run
src/components/kanban/kanban-board-layout.test.ts
src/components/kanban/__tests__/kanban-board-motion.test.ts

<!-- This is an auto-generated description by cubic. -->
---
## Summary by cubic
Animate cross-column card moves in the kanban board with a board-level
overlay and ghost cards for smooth, accessible transitions. The desktop
kanban is now normalized into a shared scene model, with layout and
motion handled in `@acepe/ui`.

- **New Features**
- Smooth travel/settle animations for cross-column moves; respects
reduced motion.
- `KanbanSceneBoard` accepts a normalized `model` (columns, cards,
placements) for deterministic ordering and motion.
- Overlay-based ghost rendering prevents layout jumps during
transitions.

- **Refactors**
- Desktop builds a `KanbanSceneModel` via `buildDesktopKanbanScene`; UI
owns layout (`kanban-board-layout`) and motion (`kanban-board-motion*`)
logic.
- Replaced brittle source-text assertions with behavioral tests in
desktop and `@acepe/ui`.
- Internal board API uses per-column `layout`; `KanbanCard` adds a ghost
presentation mode.

<sup>Written for commit cb7a07991fc5f56c7efb0863ada25864fccca23b.
Summary will update on new commits.</sup>

<!-- End of auto-generated description by cubic. -->

---------

Co-authored-by: Test User <test@example.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
